attacking_style: Commits to extremely long-range breakaways (4+ hours), succeeding on stages where terrain selectivity prevents full peloton chase organization. Patient diesel engine approach rather than explosive late attacks.
team_tactics: Relies on individual autonomy in breakaways rather than team setup; gets into early moves before sprinter teams fully commit to chase organization.
terrain_preference: Selective terrain that splits peloton attention without being climber-dominated; stages where sprinter teams hesitate on chase commitment. Exceptional time trial power sustains long solo efforts.
vulnerability: Likely exposed when GC teams or climbers aggressively control from early stages; depends on opposition miscalculation of chase requirements.
key_patterns: Executes 4+ hour solo/breakaway efforts, proven across both Catalunya stage wins (2 observations)
Wins from early breakaways on selective terrain, fourth Catalunya stage win from breaks
Success relies on sprinter team underestimation of his TT power and breakaway sustainability
Targets stages where peloton control is ambiguous between sprinter and selective terrain dynamics
form_trajectory: positive: Back-to-back stage wins demonstrating peak condition and fourth career Catalunya win from breaks shows refined tactical execution
